title: Migrate Visualizer to TypeScript
description:
# Convert Visualizer to TypeScript

## 📝 User Story / Description
The current ES6 Javascript frontend lacks design-time compilation, allowing syntax errors (`let state.X` and `const state.Y`) to slip into runtime and crash the visualizer. Converting to TypeScript provides strict structural typing and catches syntax errors before the browser launches.

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Initialize NPM package and configure `tsconfig.json` for ES6 modules.
- [ ] Move `visualizer/*.js` to `src/*.ts`.
- [ ] Fix existing syntax errors (e.g., `const state.segGap`).
- [ ] Update `bin/start-visualizer` to run `npx tsc` before launching.
- [ ] Visualizer compiles and runs cleanly.

## 🛠️ Technical Notes
- Use `npx tsc --init`.
- Setup output directory to `visualizer/`.
- Ensure no runtime exceptions in GTK WebKit view.

## 🛡️ Rigor & Verification Boundary
- [ ] Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to verify type and syntax soundness.
- [ ] `nomos verify` passes smoothly.

- **Quality Debt Exemptions:**
  - `complexity_limit: false`
  - `doc_drift: false`
  - `dry_candidate: false`
